description: run_payment_inference calls cudaDeviceSynchronize() between kernel launch and cudaGetLastError() to force runtime errors (illegal memory access on customer-influenced payloads) to surface. The other two runners launch kernels on internally-generated fixed-shape tensors where execution errors aren't reachable. Unifying onto a shared runner that drops the sync silently corrupts the GPU context on a single bad payment.

findings.rationale: run_payment_inference has an extra cudaDeviceSynchronize() call between the kernel launch and the error-check; the other two skip it. The sync is load-bearing: without it, cudaGetLastError reports only launch-time errors and runtime kernel faults silently corrupt the GPU context. extra-call shape, on critical path.
